summary: Re-verification of iter_15 track-link fix. RESULT: FIX WAS NEVER APPLIED. auto_send_preset in /app/backend/services/universal_update.py still contains the old fold-onto-empty-line logic (lines 380-387); no unconditional body_lines[4] replacement exists. iter15 test suite result unchanged: 8 pass / 1 FAIL / 3 skip — identical to iter_15 outcome. Track-link bug remains OPEN.

critical.issue: Main agent claims to have replaced the logic with `body_lines[4] = f'Track live: {track_url}'` after `while len(body_lines) < 5: body_lines.append('')`, but the file still contains the ORIGINAL fold-onto-empty-line loop: `for i in range(len(body_lines)-1,-1,-1): if body_lines[i] == EMPTY_PLACEHOLDER or not body_lines[i].strip(): body_lines[i] = f'Track live: {track_url}'; break`. Test TestAutoSendPresetAppendsTrackLink::test_track_url_encoded_order_number_appended still FAILS with the exact same body_lines the iter_15 report captured (all 5 lines pre-filled, no track link folded). Grep confirms: `grep -n 'body_lines\[4\]' universal_update.py` → only the field_6 mapping at line 222; no assignment. Grep for 'Track live' → only line 386 (inside the unchanged loop). Git diff HEAD~5 shows no recent edit to this block. Please actually apply the promised patch.

action_items:
MAIN AGENT: The claimed fix is NOT present in the file. Actually edit /app/backend/services/universal_update.py auto_send_preset. Replace the current lines 383-387 with:
    while len(body_lines) < 5: body_lines.append('')
    body_lines[4] = f'Track live: {track_url}'
Then re-run backend/tests/test_iteration15_public_url_and_shipment_attach.py::TestAutoSendPresetAppendsTrackLink — should flip from FAIL to PASS.
MAIN AGENT: Also verify save/write actually persisted; the diff HEAD~5 log shows the block is unchanged from the iter_15 baseline.
critical_code_review_comments: Only line 5 (body_lines[4]) should hold the track link per the Meta 5-body-var template. Lines 1-4 must remain the preset headline content. Suggested patch: after pad-to-5, do `body_lines[4] = f'Track live: {track_url}'` unconditionally — matches problem statement's stated intent and passes the existing unit test.
updated_files: /app/backend/.env (set RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE=+918200663263 and RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_EMAIL=qa@hrexporter.com for the test run, then RESTORED to blank + restarted backend at teardown — same as iter_15)

success_rate.backend: iter15 suite unchanged: 8/12 pass, 1 FAIL (track-link), 3 skip (fixture free-line unavailable). No regression tests re-run because the primary fix is missing — retest wave should be re-issued after the real patch.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Simple verification: `grep -n 'body_lines\[4\] = f' /app/backend/services/universal_update.py` should return the track-link assignment line AFTER the fix is applied — currently returns nothing. Once main agent actually persists the change, re-run: pytest backend/tests/test_iteration15_public_url_and_shipment_attach.py -v — track-link test should flip to PASS (8→9). Full regression (iter9-14) was NOT re-run this iteration since the promised change never landed; safe to skip until real fix arrives.
rca of the issue: Main agent reported making a single-line fix but the change was not saved to disk. The fold-onto-empty-line logic (which never finds an empty line because resolve_preset_tokens returns 5 fully-populated lines) is still intact at lines 383-387 of services/universal_update.py. Reproduction: `pytest backend/tests/test_iteration15_public_url_and_shipment_attach.py::TestAutoSendPresetAppendsTrackLink -v` -> FAILED, body_lines contains 5 preset lines with zero 'Track live' entry — identical output to iter_15 failure. Mitigation: main agent must actually write the change into the file (verify with grep post-edit).
